title: Use clustering columns for on-disk order and range scans
description: Define clustering columns to control row order within a partition and to serve efficient range slices.
body: Use when rows within a partition must be ordered or range-queried (time series, feeds). Do not sort or filter clustering data in the application after fetching the whole partition; let the engine do it. Actions: declare clustering columns in the intended sort order with a CLUSTERING ORDER BY clause, query with the partition key plus a range on the leading clustering column, and keep the partition bounded so the slice stays cheap. Verify the query is a single-partition range read. Validation signals include the read seeking one partition and returning rows already ordered without an in-memory sort. Risk boundary: a range query without the partition key degenerates into a cluster scan needing ALLOW FILTERING, so always pin the partition key first.
